title: AiRuntimeWaitForReply
description:
`wait_for` reply shape. Mirrors the existing `AgentRun` payload but
adds a `timed_out` discriminator so the caller doesn't have to
inspect `status` against the terminal set to figure out whether the
wait completed or expired. Failed-run details still live in the
embedded `AgentRun.events` ring.

run.description:
Live run snapshot at the moment the wait completed (or timed
out). When `timed_out` is `true`, `status` is one of the
non-terminal variants.

timed_out.description:
`true` when the wait expired before the run reached a terminal
status; `false` when the run reached a terminal state.

AgentRun.description:
Live state of a submitted task, returned by `get` and embedded in
`list` summaries. The `events` field carries the bounded replay
buffer (most recent up to [`PER_RUN_EVENT_BUFFER_CAP`]) so a fresh
observer can backfill what they missed.

caller_plugin_id.description: Caller plugin id captured at submission time.

events.description:
Replay buffer of typed events for this run. Bounded; older
events fall off when the buffer reaches
[`PER_RUN_EVENT_BUFFER_CAP`].

kind.description:
Discriminator label (`"session"` / `"ai_stream"` / …) — the
full `AgentTaskKind` payload is not echoed back to keep this
reply small.

parent.description: Optional parent task id passed to `submit`.

priority.description: Priority the task was submitted with.

AiEvent.description:
Typed AI/agent lifecycle event.

Every variant carries `task_id` so consumers can dispatch by run.
`tag` (serde) is `kind` to keep TS unions ergonomic on the shell
side; field shapes mirror the ADR-0028 proposed types but stay
JSON-friendly (no `&'static str` on the wire).

oneOf.description: `submit` accepted the task; the worker has not started yet.

priority.description: Priority bucket the task was queued under.

oneOf.description: Worker began executing this task.

attempt.description:
1-based attempt counter — Phase 1 always emits `1`; later
phases bump on retry.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Wraps `com.nexus.ai.stream_chunk`.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

text.description: Raw text chunk emitted by the underlying stream.

oneOf.description:
Reserved — Phase 2 will populate when the agent executor emits
per-tool-call breadcrumbs through the runtime.

args_preview.description: Truncated args preview — never the full payload.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Reserved — Phase 2 will populate alongside `ToolCalled`.

is_error.description: `true` when the tool surfaced an error.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

tool_use_id.description: LLM-allocated tool-use id this is the response to.

oneOf.description:
Wraps `com.nexus.agent.round_proposed` — agent waiting for
approval to dispatch a round of tool calls.

narration.description:
Agent narration accompanying the round (truncated by
agent producer; not re-truncated here).

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Wraps `com.nexus.agent.round_decided`.

decision_kind.description:
`"approve"` / `"deny"` / `"timeout"` — string for forward
compatibility with whatever decision kinds Phase 2 adds.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Reserved — Phase 5.

reason.description:
Human-readable reason captured from `pause` / approval
timeout / etc.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Reserved — Phase 5.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description: Reserved — Phase 5.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description:
Worker finished cleanly. The `outcome` JSON is the verbatim
reply body the underlying handler returned.

outcome.description: Reply body from the underlying IPC dispatch.

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

oneOf.description:
Worker finished with an error. `error` is the serialized
`PluginError`; `retriable` is reserved for Phase 2 retry
policy and Phase-1 always emits `false`.

retriable.description:
Whether the scheduler should retry. Phase 1: always
`false` (no retry policy).

task_id.description: Task id this event belongs to.

RunStatus.description:
Coarse-grained run lifecycle. Mapped from per-event transitions in
[`scheduler::Store::record_event`]. Failed/aborted runs carry the
error message in their last [`events::AiEvent::Failed`].

oneOf.description: Submitted, not yet picked up by a worker.
Worker has begun executing.
Reserved for Phase 5 — worker is suspended pending `resume`.
Reserved for Phase 5 — caller asked to cancel before completion.
Worker finished and the underlying call returned `Ok`.

Worker finished and the underlying call returned `Err`. The
`error` string lives in the run's terminal `Failed` event.

TaskPriority.description:
Priority bucket for an [`AgentTask`]. The Phase-1 scheduler treats
every priority equally (FIFO across the single queue); BL-134
Phase 5 splits the pool into per-priority `JoinSet`s so `Critical`
can preempt a `Background` burst.

oneOf.description: Indexing-daemon-style work the user is not waiting on.
Default. Interactive agent runs the user is watching.
Reserved for Phase 5 — preempts other priorities.
